subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Apollo 11: Walking on the Moon
description: One-minute skill: Apollo 11: Walking on the Moon.

hook.text: On July 20, 1969, Neil Armstrong stepped onto the Moon. 600 million people watched live on television.

buildup.text: Kennedy had pledged to reach the Moon by the end of the decade. NASA achieved it with just five months to spare.

discovery.text: Armstrong and Aldrin spent 2.5 hours on the surface. They collected rocks, planted a flag, and left footprints that remain today.

twist.text: The landing almost failed. Armstrong had to manually fly past a boulder field with just 25 seconds of fuel remaining.

climax.text: Twelve people walked on the Moon between 1969 and 1972. No one has returned since—but Artemis aims to change that.

punchline.text: Twelve walked on the Moon. Their footprints will last millions of years.

quiz.question: How close did Apollo 11 come to running out of fuel during the Moon landing?
options: Armstrong had only about 25 seconds of fuel left when he landed
They had plenty of fuel—the landing went exactly as planned
They ran out of fuel and had to abort the first attempt
